config.title: Oktoberfest in Berlin: FAQ
config.subtitle: Confirmed 2026 dates for Alexanderplatz, Spandau, Berlin Beer Week, Munich and the separate Herbstrummel funfair.

items.q: Is there an Oktoberfest in Berlin?
items.a: Yes. Berlin has confirmed Oktoberfest-style events at Alexanderplatz and beside Spandau Citadel in 2026, plus smaller venue events. None operates at Munich scale or shapes Berlin the way the original Wiesn shapes Munich.

items.q: When is Oktoberfest on Alexanderplatz in 2026?
items.a: Oktoberfest on Alexanderplatz runs from Friday 25 September to Sunday 4 October 2026. Entry is free. The programme includes a Paulaner beer garden, food and live entertainment. Take the S-Bahn or U2, U5 or U8 to Alexanderplatz.

items.q: When is the Spandau Citadel Oktoberfest in 2026?
items.a: The Spandau event runs from Friday 18 September to Saturday 24 October 2026, but only on Fridays and Saturdays. The tent has more than 2,000 seats. Take U7 to Zitadelle and check the organizer for the current admission, programme and reservation packages.

items.q: When is Berlin Beer Week in 2026?
items.a: Berlin Beer Week runs from Friday 28 August to Sunday 6 September 2026. It is a distributed programme across breweries, bars and boats, not one street festival. The organizer lists more than 35 events, over 100 breweries and six Brews Cruises; admission varies by event.

items.q: Is Berliner Herbstrummel the Berlin Oktoberfest?
items.a: No. Berliner Herbstrummel is a separate autumn funfair at Zentraler Festplatz, running from Friday 25 September to Sunday 25 October 2026. The separate Oktoberfest listing at the same fairground still has no confirmed 2026 date, so do not merge the two events when planning.

items.q: What happened to the Internationales Berliner Bierfestival on Karl-Marx-Allee?
items.a: The former Biermeile last ran in 2019. In February 2020 its organizer announced that it would not continue. Third-party pages advertising a 2026 edition on Karl-Marx-Allee are not current official event confirmation.

items.q: When is Munich Oktoberfest 2026 and can I visit from Berlin?
items.a: Munich Oktoberfest runs from Saturday 19 September to Sunday 4 October 2026 on the Theresienwiese. The fastest direct trains can take around four hours each way, so it is better as an overnight trip than a spontaneous day out. Check Deutsche Bahn and the official Oktoberfest site before booking.

items.q: What should I drink or where should I go outside the event dates?
items.a: Try a Berliner Weisse mit raspberry or woodruff syrup, or check same-day opening hours for Prater Garten, Café am Neuen See or Zollpackhof. Berlin beer gardens are seasonal and weather-dependent, so do not assume they open every day of the year.
